Brand StandardsManual

“Men Edifying Others”

OrganizationSigma Rhomeo Incorporated
Founded1980 · Prairie View A&M
EditionModern Standards, Version III
AuthorityThe Grand Council
01Our Identity

One Order, One Voice

The Sigma Rhomeo brand is the reputation the Order has built since 1980. It carries our name, our marks, and the standard our members are held to. Every flyer, jersey, page, and post is a point of contact, and each one either strengthens that reputation or weakens it. This manual exists so that every chapter and every vendor presents the Order with one face and one voice.

How To Name The Order

The name has three approved forms. Choose the form that fits the context, and keep the trademark symbols intact in graphical use.

FormWhen to useMark
The Fraternal Order of Classy Gentlemen: Sigma Rhomeo IncorporatedFirst reference, legal, ceremonial, and formal documents™ in graphical use
Sigma Rhomeo, Inc.Subsequent references in body copy and correspondence—
Sigma RhomeoConversational and editorial references—
Trademark rule. When the full name appears in graphical form, it must carry the ™ symbol. The Coat of Arms and the official Seals must carry the registered ® symbol in all media. Spell the name correctly and never abbreviate it to slang or a nickname.

Names That Are Never Used

The Order's name and Greek letters may not be deconstructed, reassembled, or paired with anything that places the Order in an unfavorable light. The following are prohibited in every medium: Classy Gents, The Meo's, Freaky Skeeters, Classy Rhomeos, and any use of “Skeet-Skeet” alongside the Coat of Arms, symbols, or logos.

Who This Manual Serves

Members & Chapters

Officers and brothers producing apparel, flyers, programs, and chapter media.

Licensed Vendors

Designers and manufacturers creating products that carry Sigma Rhomeo marks.

The Grand Council

National leadership, the Multimedia Team, and Communications stewards who approve and protect the brand.

02Brand Foundation

What We Stand For

The brand rests on a clear purpose: principled men who make the people and places around them better. The statements below are drafted from the Order's motto, the symbolism of the Coat of Arms, and the editorial standards of The Eternal Knight. They are written for ratification by the Grand Council and should be confirmed as official before publication.

Mission · proposed

Sigma Rhomeo develops principled men who edify others through leadership, scholarship, mentorship, and service to their communities.

Vision · proposed

A brotherhood recognized in every community for character, achievement, and the steady elevation of those around them.

Core Values

Six values carry the Order's character. They trace directly to the Coat of Arms and to the four strengths named in our standards: fortitude, dedication, integrity, and brotherhood.

Honor

The knight's code. We keep our word and protect the Order's name.

Integrity

We hold the line in private and in public, on and off the record.

Scholarship

The book and quill. We pursue knowledge and raise the standard.

Mentorship

We lift the next man and the next generation behind us.

Service

The torch. We carry our gifts into the community and leave it better.

Brotherhood

The bond. We stand together and answer for one another.

Positioning Statement · proposed

For men who hold themselves to a higher standard, Sigma Rhomeo is the fraternal order that turns brotherhood into measurable good, edifying others through disciplined leadership, scholarship, and service.

Brand Personality

Five traits set the tone for everything the Order produces. Dignified, never stiff. Principled, never preachy. Honorable, in the tradition of the knight. Professional, in print and in person. Forward thinking, while honoring those who came before.

Brand Story

Sigma Rhomeo began in 1980 at Prairie View A&M with a charge captured in three words: Men Edifying Others. The knight became the face of that charge, a figure of honor and integrity who kneels not in defeat but in service. The Coat of Arms followed in 1983 and has grown alongside the Order ever since. What started as a brotherhood on one campus now carries a name worth protecting in every community it touches. This manual keeps that name strong, consistent, and unmistakably ours.

03Heritage

A Legacy Worth Protecting

Our marks carry history. The Order was founded in 1980 at Prairie View A&M, and the Coat of Arms was adopted in 1983. The arms have been refined across the years to match the Order's growth, while the blazon and its meaning have stayed constant. Understanding the heritage of each element is the first step to using it correctly.

The Evolution Of The Arms

The earlier heraldic rendering and the current refined Coat of Arms share the same blazon: the same cross pattée, helmet, stars, torch, book, and scroll. Refinement has sharpened the drawing and standardized the color. It has never changed the meaning. When you reproduce the arms, use only current approved files. Never rebuild or redraw them.

The 1981 Original Shield

This is the Order's original shield, preserved for the historical record. It predates the 1983 Coat of Arms. The artwork shows a lit torch above a shield bearing a bend set with roundels, a mailed arm grasping a thunderbolt, an open book with a quill, and a knight's helm, above a scroll carrying the founding year and the original lettering.

Archive and esoteric use only. The 1981 Original Shield is retained for the historical record and for esoteric, members-only use within the Order. It is never used in public, commercial, digital, promotional, apparel, or chapter settings, and it does not replace the current Coat of Arms. Any reproduction requires Grand Council approval.

The Knight

The knight is the mascot and the spirit of the Order. He represents honor and integrity, and he is always a man. He always faces dexter, to the viewer's left, to signal forward motion. Never reverse his direction, and never render him as a cartoon, an animal, or a childlike figure. Whenever the knight appears with our name or symbols, he meets the same standards as every other mark.

The Greek Letters

The Order's Greek letters are Sigma, Rho, Mu, Epsilon, Omicron, spelled in succession as Sigma Rhomeo. In graphical use the proprietary form pairs a Sigma with a roman character “R” to read as “Sigma R.” The Sigma and the R must be identical in style. Mixing typefaces in the letters weakens the brand and is not allowed.

The Motto

Men Edifying Others

Seven golden stars, four above and three below, crown the kneeling knight.

04Visual Identity

The System At A Glance

Sigma Rhomeo communicates through a family of marks, two heraldic colors, and a disciplined type system. Each mark has a job. Using the right mark for the right setting is the difference between a polished presentation and a diluted one.

The Family Of Marks

MarkPrimary useWho may use it
Official Coat of Arms (navy crest, steel helmet)Official fraternal order business. Recognition awards and ceremony.Grand Council only
Primary Coat of Arms (full color, gold field)The everyday crest for web, advertising, and public communication.All chapters and members
1981 Original Shield (historical)The original shield, preserved for the record. Never public.Archive and esoteric use only
Logomark (crest + wordmark + motto)Official and formal settings, public facing communication.All chapters and members
WordmarkName in the official typeface, with or without the motto.All chapters and members
Knight Head logoDigital avatars, icons, two-color applications.All chapters and members
Kneeling Knight logoSingle-color emblem, apparel, spirit marks.All chapters and members
Fraternity SealAuthenticity and matters of urgency.Grand Council only
Seal of the National PresidentAuthenticity of presidential documents.National President only

The Order Of Honor

The marks are not interchangeable. The Coat of Arms exists in two approved renderings. The Official Coat of Arms, the navy crest with the steel helmet, is reserved for official fraternal order business and the Grand Council. The Primary Coat of Arms, the full color crest on the gold field, is the everyday mark for web, advertising, and public communication. The Logomark and Wordmark carry public communication. The Knight icons carry digital and informal use. The Seals are restricted instruments of authority and never appear on apparel, public publications, or websites.

Primary Coat of Arms
Knight Head
Kneeling Knight
Fraternity Seal
05Logo System

Logos & Lockups

The Logomark is the public face of the Order. It combines the logo, the wordmark, and the motto. It may appear in Royal Blue, Antique Gold, or Athletic Gold. When the official colors are not available, reproduce it in black. Never hot-stamp or treat the Logomark with metallic inks, and avoid foil stamping or die cutting where production quality cannot be controlled.

Primary Wordmark

The name set in the official typeface. Carries the ™ symbol in graphical use.

Logomark Lockups

Horizontal lockup · light backgrounds
Reversed lockup · dark backgrounds

The Coat of Arms may stand in place of the Knight logo within the Logomark for formal settings. The motto may be removed when space is tight. Never replace the motto with a chapter designation or a university name. A chapter line does not advance the unified brand, and a university name infringes on the institution's rights.

Knight Marks For Digital

Knight Head · gold
Two color
Kneeling Knight · blue
Single color

Clear Space & Minimum Size

  • Hold clear space around the Logomark equal to the height of the “A” in the wordmark.
  • Hold clear space around the Coat of Arms equal to one quarter inch, or to scale.
  • The Coat of Arms, Wordmark, and Logomark print no smaller than 1¼ inches wide or tall.
  • Any logo printed alone holds a minimum of ½ inch.
  • The Fraternity Seal prints between 1 and 2½ inches in diameter.
  • The Seal of the National President prints between 1¾ and 3¼ inches tall.

Logo Misuse

The same discipline that protects the Coat of Arms protects every logo. Do not outline, recolor, distort, rotate, condense, or stretch the marks. Do not set the wordmark in the wrong typeface or add unapproved verbiage.

×
Do not recolor
×
Do not distort
×
Do not rotate
06Color Standards

The Palette

Two colors define Sigma Rhomeo: Royal Blue and Antique Gold. Midnight Blue serves as an acceptable secondary, and Athletic Gold is the ceremonial alternate reserved for the official Seals. Specify Pantone for spot printing, and use the values below for every other medium.

Primary · 60%
Royal Blue

Conversion note. The 2013 edition listed a single CMYK formula for all colors, which is a transcription error. The CMYK values above are converted from the official RGB for accurate four-color printing. Athletic Gold is given as a Pantone bridge approximation. Confirm against a Pantone chip before a spot run.

Usage Ratio

Lead with blue. Let Royal Blue or Midnight Blue carry roughly sixty percent of any layout, gold roughly thirty percent as accent and emphasis, and neutral paper or white the final ten percent. Gold is a highlight, not a field color for long passages of text.

Accessibility & Contrast

  • White text on Royal Blue or Midnight Blue passes contrast for body and headings. This is the preferred reversed pairing.
  • Antique Gold text on white fails contrast. Use gold only on dark fields, or as a rule, border, or accent.
  • Ink navy text on paper or white is the standard reading pairing for long-form content.
  • Never set gold body text on a light ground, and never set blue body text on a gold ground at small sizes.

Document Neutrals

For layout and typography the brand uses a small set of neutrals that are not heraldic colors. Ink Navy #10162E for text, Paper #EEF0F4 for document grounds, white for cards, and a hairline rule at #D8DCE6. These support the palette and never replace it.

07Typography

Type System

Type carries the personality of the Order: regal, clean, professional, and bold. Trajan Pro is the primary typeface for official print and brand materials. Cataneo Light BT carries the motto with a calligraphic feel. The Greek letters use Symbol and Times New Roman or an approved custom appliqué. For digital and web, where the licensed faces are unavailable, the alternates below preserve the same character.

Primary · Trajan Pro

SIGMA RHOMEO

A serif of Roman inscriptional capitals. Regal, clean, and bold. Use whenever possible for names, titles, and headings. Trajan is an all-capital face. Do not attempt lowercase.

Secondary · Cataneo Light BT

Men Edifying Others

A calligraphic companion for the motto and the former name. Pair it with Trajan. Never use it as a primary typeface.

Approved Digital Alternates

RoleBrand typefaceDigital / web alternateFallback
Display & headingsTrajan ProCinzelGeorgia
Motto & calligraphicCataneo Light BTCormorant Garamond ItalicGeorgia Italic
Body & long form—SpectralGeorgia
Labels, data, captions—InterArial / Helvetica
Greek lettersSymbol + Times New RomanSymbol + Times New Romanapproved appliqué

Hierarchy

LevelTypefaceWeightSize / trackingCase
H1Cinzel / Trajan Pro70038–62 px · +20Uppercase
H2Cinzel / Trajan Pro70026–38 px · +15Uppercase
H3Cinzel / Trajan Pro60018 px · +40Uppercase
BodySpectral40016–17 px / 1.6Sentence
Caption / labelInter500–60010–12 px · +140Uppercase
Greek letter rule. The Sigma and the “R” must be identical in style. Emphasize the Sigma R on paraphernalia unless the full name is used. Never type the Sigma R in documents or correspondence. Greek symbols are graphical, for logos, apparel, and event marketing. RHOMEO may be spelled out and stand alone in all capitals in any legible non-script face.
08Heraldry Standards

The Coat Of Arms

The Coat of Arms was adopted in 1983 and is our most honored symbol. It exists in two approved renderings. Use the right one for the setting, and never recolor one to imitate the other.

Two Approved Renderings

Official Coat of Arms

The navy and gold crest with the steel helmet. Reserved for official fraternal order business and the Grand Council: ceremony, recognition awards, and documents of authority. Not for general chapter, web, or advertising use.

Primary Coat of Arms

The full color crest on the gold field. This is the everyday mark for websites, advertising, apparel, and public communication. Chapters and members use this mark.

Choosing between them. If the work represents official fraternal order business or carries the authority of the Grand Council, use the Official Coat of Arms. For every public, web, advertising, and chapter use, use the Primary Coat of Arms. The blazon, sizing, and misuse rules below apply equally to both.

The Blazon

  • Escutcheon bearing the Royal Blue Cross Pattée on Antique Gold.
  • Helmet dexter to the bearer's right, closed visor.
  • Mantling for protection, Antique Gold.
  • Greek symbols Sigma R, in bend.
  • Arm and lightning facing sinister, embowed, clenching.
  • Scroll emblazoned with 1980, the founding date at Prairie View A&M.
  • Sword in saltire. Lance upright.
  • Book and quill open, with quill bending sinister.
  • Seven golden stars four above, three below.
  • Torch of Sigma upright, always at full flame.
  • Gold roundel bearing “Fraternal Order of Classy Gentlemen.” Blue star five points, upright.

Media Usage Rules

  • The Official Coat of Arms (navy crest, steel helmet) is for official fraternal order business and the Grand Council only.
  • The Primary Coat of Arms (full color, gold field) is the public mark. For single-color reproduction, use gold on blue, blue on gold, or either color on white.
  • Where official colors are unavailable, reproduce with a black or white outline. Blind emboss or foil stamp in blue, silver, or gold is permitted.
  • The arms may not be deconstructed. No single charge may stand alone.
  • The arms may not be covered, shown in part, or silhouetted. Display them in full.
  • In any document or website, the arms appear once: at the beginning, the middle, or the end. On letterhead, no other instance may appear.
  • On clothing, the arms are worn facing forward on the chest or left pocket, never on a sleeve or back.
  • The registered ® symbol must accompany the Coat of Arms in all media.

Unacceptable Use

These rules apply to both renderings of the arms. Do not silhouette, recolor, rotate, distort, reverse, or conceal any part. Display the crest in full, in its approved colors.

×
Do not silhouette
×
Do not use unauthorized colors
×
Do not rotate
×
Do not distort
×
Do not reverse
×
Do not conceal any part
Reproduction. Never attempt to rebuild the arms. High resolution and vector files are obtained only by direct contact with the licensed vendor and may not be dispersed to non-financial members. Request the correct files from the Multimedia Team.
09Photography

Photo Ethic

Imagery is part of the brand. The Order is represented by successful men who show the best in fortitude, dedication, integrity, and brotherhood. Whenever possible, use large, positive photographs that depict these four strengths, and avoid any image that works against them.

Style & Treatment

  • Favor natural light, clean backgrounds, and confident, dignified posture.
  • Shoot in landscape for features and headers. Keep faces sharp and well lit.
  • Color grade toward true tones. A subtle cool cast complements Royal Blue.
  • For duotone treatments, use Royal Blue and Antique Gold only, applied with restraint.

Composition

  • Lead with people in action: service, mentorship, ceremony, achievement.
  • Hold breathing room around subjects so the image can crop to several formats.
  • Show the chapter and community context, not staged excess.
  • Caption with names, dates, and place for the archival record.

Do Not Publish

  • Photos with alcohol or that indicate its use, including common containers, funnels, or games.
  • Images of illegal activity or behavior inconsistent with the values of the Order.
  • Explicit or vulgar content, including nudity and profanity on clothing or signage.
  • Confidential chapter or Fraternity information, or anything depicting the Rituals.
10Graphic Elements

Supporting Devices

The heraldry gives the brand a set of supporting graphic elements. Used sparingly, they add texture and continuity. Used heavily, they crowd the marks. Treat them as accents that frame the work, never as substitutes for the official logos.

Cross Pattée

The Royal Blue cross on gold from the escutcheon. Use as a section divider or quiet background motif at low contrast.

Seven Stars

Four above, three below. Use the star arc as a header flourish above the kneeling knight.

Torch of Sigma

Always upright and at full flame. A mark of service and knowledge.

Sword in Saltire

Crossed swords signal honor and readiness. Keep the saltire balanced and upright.

Book & Quill

Scholarship. Open book with the quill bending sinister.

Lance

Upright, behind the shield. A heraldic accent, never a standalone logo.

Rule. No single charge from the Coat of Arms may stand alone as a logo. These elements support a layout. They do not replace the wordmark, logomark, or arms.

Pattern & Texture

For backgrounds, a tiled cross pattée or a low-opacity star field in Royal Blue gives depth without noise. Hold patterns under ten percent opacity behind text, and never run a pattern across a logo or seal.

11Voice & Messaging

How The Order Speaks

The Order's voice is honorable, professional, and historically grounded, with a forward look. It is visionary without losing its footing, educational without lecturing, and authentic in every line. Every message reinforces leadership, integrity, scholarship, mentorship, and service while reflecting the dignity of the Order.

Voice Principles

Honorable

We speak with the weight of our word and our history.

Professional

Clean, accurate, and polished in every channel.

Grounded

Claims are factual, attributed, and true to the record.

Visionary

We point forward while honoring those who came first.

Educational

We inform and develop, never talk down.

Respectful

We respect members, partners, and the wider community.

Key Messages

  • Men Edifying Others. The motto leads. Every program ties back to lifting the people around us.
  • One Order, one voice. Chapters differ in place, not in standard.
  • Founded 1980. A legacy of character, achievement, and service.

Do

  • Use the approved name forms and keep trademark symbols intact.
  • Write in active voice with plain, confident verbs.
  • Attribute facts, figures, and history accurately.
  • Lead with the motto and the values it carries.

Do Not

  • Use slang names or deconstructed monikers for the Order.
  • Pair the name with profanity, partisan politics, or grievance.
  • Alter or embellish the Order's history.
  • Disclose Ritual content or confidential information.
12Digital Standards

Marks On Screen

Digital use follows the same standards as print, with a few additions for the screen. Use only approved files. Never rebuild a logo in code or from a screenshot. Hold clear space, respect minimum sizes, and choose the mark that fits the surface.

File Formats

UseFormatNotes
Web, scalableSVGPreferred for logos and the wordmark
Web, rasterPNGTransparent background, 2x for retina
PrintEPS / PDFVector, from the licensed vendor
Avatars / faviconsPNG / ICOKnight Head logo, two color

Avatars & Favicons

On blue
On gold
Monochrome
Reversed

The Knight Head logo is the standard profile and favicon mark. It reads cleanly at small sizes where the full Coat of Arms would not. The gold-field and blue-field badges suit colored profile rings, and the monochrome version suits single-color use. Keep it two color and never crop the plume or stars. See Logo System for the full set of Knight marks.

Email Signature

FIRST LAST
Chapter Office · Chapter Name
The Fraternal Order of Classy Gentlemen: Sigma Rhomeo Incorporated™
Men Edifying Others

Keep signatures text based with one small Knight Head or wordmark. Do not place the Coat of Arms or any Seal in routine email signatures.

13Stationery

Correspondence

Official stationery presents the Order at its most formal. Letterhead, cards, and envelopes follow a consistent system so that any document is recognizable as ours at a glance.

Letterhead

  • Logomark or wordmark at the top, in Royal Blue.
  • Where the Coat of Arms appears on letterhead, it appears once and no other instance is added.
  • Body type in a clean serif at 11 to 12 point, ample margins.
  • National headquarters address and website in the footer.

Business Cards

  • Knight Head or wordmark on the front, name and office in Inter.
  • Royal Blue card with gold accents, or white with blue and gold.
  • Motto set in the calligraphic alternate, small and refined.
  • Minimum logo size respected even at card scale.
Headquarters. 701 Commerce Street, Suite 620, Dallas, Texas 75202 · www.sigmarhomeoinc.org
14Apparel

Worn With Honor

Apparel carries the brand into public view more than any other medium. Placement and color rules protect the marks, and licensing protects the Order. Products that carry Sigma Rhomeo marks may be produced or sold only under a current written license from the Grand Council, even by members and chapters.

Placement

  • The Coat of Arms is worn facing forward on the chest or left pocket.
  • The arms are never placed on a sleeve or on the back of a garment.
  • The Kneeling Knight and Greek letters are suited to chests, backs, and spirit wear.
  • Keep clear space around any mark so it reads cleanly on fabric.

Color & Letters

  • Reproduce the arms in two color or one color for apparel. Full color is Grand Council only.
  • The Sigma R may appear on apparel and event marketing, but never in official correspondence.
  • Embroidery uses the proprietary Sigma and roman R. Request the correct letterform from the Multimedia Team.
  • Do not pair marks with copyrighted characters, alcohol, drugs, or profanity.
Spirit mark · one color
Reversed on blue
Chest crest · Primary Coat of Arms
Licensing. A member contacts a licensed vendor, the vendor submits the design to the Multimedia Team, and production proceeds only after written approval. Unlicensed production of branded goods violates the Order's rights.
15Regalia

Pins, Badges & Officer Regalia

Regalia is evidence of membership and office. It is worn, not displayed as decoration. The standards here protect the dignity of the badge and the authority of officer marks.

The Pledge Pin, also the Badge

  • Evidence of membership for an individual, used only for that purpose except as the laws of the Order allow.
  • Worn over the heart, never on a removable article of clothing.
  • Never used as decoration on stationery, programs, or publications outside the Order.
  • The full color pin appears only in Ritual books detailing its esoteric value.

Blazon

  • Shield and bend Royal Blue shield, Antique Gold bend.
  • Torch upright, Royal Blue flame, Antique Gold sconce at full flame.
  • Lance in pile throughout, Royal Blue, behind the shield.
Officer regalia. Office marks, such as the National President emblem, denote authority and are worn or used only by the officer who holds that station.
16Ceremonial Identity

The Order In Ceremony

Ceremony is where the marks carry their deepest meaning. The Coat of Arms, the Seals, the badge, and the Greek letters appear in their most formal dress, and confidentiality is held with care.

Use In Ceremony

  • The full color Coat of Arms is appropriate to Grand Council ceremony and recognition.
  • The badge may be presented to a mother, wife, sister, or fiancée during an Omega Rhomeo Service or formal Membership Intake.
  • Ceremonial print uses the official colors, with Athletic Gold permitted on Seals.
  • The presidential Seal authenticates documents of office and is not a decorative element.

Confidentiality

  • Ritual content is never published, posted, or described in public media.
  • The full color pin appears only in Ritual books, never in public materials.
  • Confidential chapter and Fraternity information stays within the Order.
17Official Seals

Instruments Of Authority

The Seals are restricted. They denote matters of urgency and authenticate official documents. They never appear on websites, general membership apparel, or publications intended for a non-member audience. The registered ® symbol accompanies the Seals in all media.

Fraternity Seal

Used only by the Grand Council. Reproduced in Royal Blue and Antique Gold, or Athletic Gold. In black where official colors are unavailable. Prints between 1 and 2½ inches in diameter.

Seal of the National President

Used only by the National President to authenticate documents. Reproduced in the official colors or Athletic Gold, in black where colors are unavailable. Prints between 1¾ and 3¼ inches tall.

Restriction. The Seals are not for web use, general apparel, or audiences outside the membership. Blind emboss or foil stamp in silver or gold is permitted. The ® symbol is required in every use.
18Social Media

Social Channels

Social media shapes how the public sees the Order day to day. Every account reflects on the whole brotherhood, so the graphic standards of this manual apply in full to profiles, posts, and stories. Present an image that would make our founders, our families, and the Grand Council proud.

Profile Standards

  • Use the Knight Head logo as the profile image, two color, never cropped or recolored.
  • Use the Logomark or a brand-colored banner for cover images. The Coat of Arms appears once where it is used.
  • Name accounts with the chapter and the Order's approved name, never a slang form.
  • Keep bios accurate, with the motto and a link to the chapter or national site.

Channels & Roles

ChannelBest for
InstagramPhotography, service, ceremony, achievement features
FacebookEvents, alumni reach, community programs
XDay to day updates, reminders, public chapter news
LinkedInProfessional achievement and officer leadership
YouTubeRecruitment films and educational content
TikTokShort form storytelling within the standards

Content Discipline

  • No alcohol, drugs, illegal activity, profanity, or explicit content.
  • No deconstructed names, slang monikers, or marks paired with copyrighted characters.
  • No unmoderated comment threads or boards without controlled access.
  • No member contact details or confidential information without consent.
19Website Standards

Chapter Websites

Chapter sites present public information about the chapter and every member of it. Design them tastefully, follow all local, state, and federal law, and abide by the policies of the Order. The graphic standards of this manual apply to websites and social pages alike.

Approved Content

  • Chapter leadership, contacts, and recruitment overview.
  • Calendar of events, good news, scholarship and service results.
  • History, objectives, symbols, and notable members.
  • Links to the host institution and to Fraternity headquarters.

Inappropriate Content

  • Changes to the history of the Order, or Ritual information.
  • Alcohol, drugs, illegal activity, or offensive media.
  • Confidential information, or member contact details without consent.
  • Trademarks used without consent, or political and identity endorsements.
Coat of Arms on the web. Use the two-color arms only. It may appear once, at the top of the main page. Sites that fall out of compliance may lose the right to link to the national site and may receive a notice to correct or remove the offending material.
20Vendor Standards

Licensing & Approval

Vendors are partners in protecting the brand. Only licensed vendors may produce goods carrying Sigma Rhomeo marks, and every custom design passes through approval before production. Never rebuild a logo. Use only approved files supplied by the licensed source.

Custom Design Approval

StepAction
1A member contacts a licensed vendor to create a customized product.
2The vendor develops a new design or accepts a design from the member.
3The vendor submits the design to the Multimedia Team for approval.
4If the design meets the standards, the Multimedia Team approves it.
5If not, the design returns to the vendor with the required changes.
File access. High resolution and vector copies are obtained only by direct contact with the vendor. They are not used by general membership or dispersed to non-financial members.
Become licensed. To become a licensed vendor, contact the Financial Committee at cfo@sigmarhomeoinc.org. Design approvals are handled by multimedia@sigmarhomeoinc.org.
21Brand Governance

Stewardship & Authority

The brand is one of the Order's most valuable assets, and it is managed with care. Governance defines who approves what, who protects the marks, and how the standards stay current.

Roles

RoleAuthority
Grand CouncilOwns the marks. Approves brand policy and the full color Coat of Arms and Seals.
National CommunicationsStewards voice, messaging, and The Eternal Knight.
Multimedia TeamApproves custom designs, holds the master files, and issues correct letterforms.
Chapter Brand StewardsApply the standards locally and route designs for approval.

Enforcement

Sigma Rhomeo maintains exclusive rights to its insignia and trademarks, registered with the federal government. Members may use approved marks within these guidelines. Unauthorized use is a violation of law and may result in a notice to correct, removal of material, or litigation. Use marks only with the consent and within the standards set by the Grand Council.

Version Control

This edition refreshes the 2013 Official Graphics Manual into a modern standard. It preserves every rule of the original and adds digital, social, ceremonial, and editorial guidance. Submit corrections and questions to National Communications, and confirm proposed statements with the Grand Council before they are published as official.

Brand questions. Multimedia and approvals: multimedia@sigmarhomeoinc.org · Licensing: cfo@sigmarhomeoinc.org · Headquarters: 701 Commerce Street, Suite 620, Dallas, Texas 75202.
22Publications & Editorial

The Eternal Knight

The Eternal Knight is the official quarterly publication of the Order. It is the historical record, the leadership voice, and the showcase of member achievement. Each issue educates, informs, inspires, and preserves the legacy of the Order while documenting its growth.

Publication Identity

  • Type Official quarterly magazine.
  • Schedule Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall.
  • Publisher Sigma Rhomeo Incorporated.
  • Audience Members, alumni, prospects, partners, and friends of the Order.

Cover Standards

  • The Eternal Knight masthead and the official wordmark.
  • Volume number, issue number, season, and year.
  • A primary feature photograph and up to three headlines.
  • The official website and an optional QR code to the digital edition.

Editorial Voice

The Eternal Knight speaks in a voice that is honorable, professional, historically grounded, visionary, educational, inspirational, authentic, respectful, and forward thinking. Every article reinforces leadership, integrity, scholarship, mentorship, and service while reflecting the dignity of the Order.

Visual Style

  • Use the official Royal Blue and Antique Gold palette and the official type hierarchy.
  • Use professional photography, clean editorial layouts, and consistent iconography.
  • Hold white space for readability, and use high quality infographics where they help.

Special Editions

National Communications may publish special editions, including the National Conclave Edition, the Annual Report Edition, the Founders' Day Commemorative Edition, the Leadership Conference Edition, the Historical Anniversary Edition, and the Memorial Tribute Edition. Each follows the same editorial and visual standards while expanding content appropriate to the occasion.

“Men Edifying Others”

THE FRATERNAL ORDER OF CLASSY GENTLEMEN: SIGMA RHOMEO INCORPORATED™

This brand standards manual protects the name, marks, and reputation the Order has built since 1980. Follow these standards consistently, and the Order presents one face and one voice in every community it touches. Proposed mission, vision, positioning, and value statements await ratification by the Grand Council before they are published as official.
